---
title: "Updated DC Crime Data"
author: "Ian Sargent"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Packages
```{r}
# Load packages with pacman library
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation
  sf, # Working with simple feature geometries 
  janitor # Column name cleaning
)
```


## Loading Datasets

#### Census
```{r}
# Loading geospatial census data LOCALLY (Due to size constraints)
# NOTE: I believe this one is located in our Google Drive "Data" folder
census <- st_read("Census_ACS_new.geojson") |> 
  # Put into wide format (Each column = a variable)
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(tract, year, geometry),
    names_from = variable,
    values_from = estimate
  ) |> 
  # Just using years 2020 and later due to tract boundary changes that year
  filter(year >= 2020) |>
  # Standardizing "tract" column for joins
  mutate(tract = str_replace_all(tract, ";", ",")) |>
  # Creating rate variables (better than raw population counts)
  mutate(
    # Race / Ethnicity Variables
    pct_white        = non_hispanic_white / total_population,
    pct_black_nh     = non_hispanic_black / total_population,
    pct_hispanic     = hispanic_latino    / total_population,
    
    # Education Variables
    pct_bachelors_only = bachelors_degree / total_population,
    pct_graduate       = (masters_degree + professional_degree + doctorate_degree) / total_population,
    
    # Socioeconomic Variables
    pct_poverty      = population_below_poverty / total_population,
    pct_unemployed   = unemployed_population    / total_population,
    pct_single_parent = single_parent_households / total_households,
    
    # Housing Variables
    pct_renter       = renter_occupied_units / total_housing_units,
    pct_vacant       = vacant_units          / total_housing_units,
    
    # Log population for offset
    log_pop          = log(total_population)
  ) |> 
  # Dropping raw count variables (We won't need those anymore)
  dplyr::select(-c(
    non_hispanic_white, non_hispanic_black, hispanic_latino,
    bachelors_degree, masters_degree, professional_degree, doctorate_degree,
    population_below_poverty, unemployed_population,
    vacant_units, renter_occupied_units, owner_occupied_units,
    single_parent_households
  ))

# Ensure the coordinate system is the same (for distance calculations)
census <- st_transform(census, crs=4326)
```


#### Crime Incidents
```{r}
# Load Crime Incident Datasets by Year (2020 - 2024) from GitHub
years <- 2020:2024
crime <- map_dfr(years, ~{
  url <- paste0("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iansargent/DC-Metro-Crime/main/Data/Crime/Crime_Incidents_in_", .x, ".geojson")
  st_read(url) |> mutate(year = .x)
})

# Ensure the coordinate system is the same (for distance calculations)
crime <- st_transform(crime, crs=4326)
```


#### Metro Stations
```{r}
# Load Geospatial DC Metro Stations from GitHub
metro <- st_read(dsn = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iansargent/DC-Metro-Crime/refs/heads/main/Data/Metro/Metros_Ground_Transfer.geoJson") |> 
  # Select the relevant columns
  dplyr::select(c(NAME, LINE, ADDRESS, GIS_ID, Above_Ground, Transfer_Station, geometry)) |> 
  # Standardize column names to lowercase
  janitor::clean_names()

# Ensure the coordinate system is the same (for distance calculations)
metro <- st_transform(metro, crs=4326)
```


### Tract Centroids to Nearest Metro Station Distance
```{r, warning=FALSE} 
# Define Tract Centroids (using the 2020 boundaries)
tract_centroids <- st_centroid(census |> filter(year == 2020)) 

# Standardize CRS for distance variables
tract_centroids <- st_transform(tract_centroids, crs=26918)
metro_projected <- st_transform(metro, crs=26918)

# Distance from each centroid to all metro stations
dist_matrix_tracts <- st_distance(tract_centroids, metro_projected)

# Assign nearest station info to tracts
nearest_idx_tracts <- apply(dist_matrix_tracts, 1, which.min)
tract_centroids$dist_to_nearest_metro <- apply(dist_matrix_tracts, 1, min)
tract_centroids$nearest_station_id <- metro_projected$gis_id[nearest_idx_tracts]

# Define Distance Buffer Variables (400m and 800m)
tract_centroids$stations_within_400m <- rowSums(dist_matrix_tracts <= units::set_units(400, "m"))
tract_centroids$stations_within_800m <- rowSums(dist_matrix_tracts <= units::set_units(800, "m"))

# Join station attributes with
tract_proximity_to_metro <- tract_centroids |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(metro_projected), by = c("nearest_station_id" = "gis_id")) |>
  dplyr::select(tract, dist_to_nearest_metro, name, nearest_station_id, stations_within_400m, stations_within_800m, line, above_ground, transfer_station)

glimpse(tract_proximity_to_metro)
```


## Joining Tables
```{r}
# Join Crime Incidents With Census (Incidents Within Each Tract)
tract_crime <- st_join(crime, census, join = st_within)

# Clean the Dataset
crimes_census <- tract_crime |> 
  # Remove rows where years don't match up
  filter(year.x == year.y) |> 
  filter(!is.na(tract)) |> 
  # Rename the year column
  rename("year" = "year.y")|> 
  # Remove unnecessary variables
  dplyr::select(-c(BLOCK, BLOCK_GROUP, WARD, ANC, DISTRICT, PSA, VOTING_PRECINCT, BID, OCTO_RECORD_ID, year.x)) |> 
  # Filter to 2020 and above due to new census tract boundaries
  filter(year >= 2020) |>
  # Clean column names to lowercase
  janitor::clean_names() |> 
  # Standardize tract column
  mutate(tract = str_replace_all(tract, ";", ",")) |>
  # Sort by tract name
  arrange(tract) |> 
  left_join(tract_proximity_to_metro, by = "tract")
```


### Distance to Metro Stations
```{r}
# Ensure CRS of Combined and Metro Datasets
metro_proj <- st_transform(metro, crs = 26918)

# Combined Master Dataset
master <- crimes_census |>
  left_join(st_drop_geometry(metro_proj), by = c("nearest_station_id" = "gis_id")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(xblock, yblock, objectid, address, census_tract, neighborhood_cluster, line.y, above_ground.y, transfer_station.y, name.y)) |> 
  mutate(
    # Extract month variable
    month = month(report_dat),
    # Extract season variable
    season = case_when(
      month %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
      month %in% c(3, 4, 5)  ~ "Spring",
      month %in% c(6, 7, 8)  ~ "Summer",
      month %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"),
    # Metro line dummy variables
    red_line = as.integer(str_detect(line.x, "red")),
    green_line = as.integer(str_detect(line.x, "green")),
    yellow_line = as.integer(str_detect(line.x, "yellow")),
    blue_line = as.integer(str_detect(line.x, "blue")),
    orange_line = as.integer(str_detect(line.x, "orange")),
    silver_line = as.integer(str_detect(line.x, "silver"))
  ) |> 
  # Rename columns
  rename(
    "line" = "line.x",
    "transfer_station" = "transfer_station.x",
    "above_ground" = "above_ground.x",
    "nearest_station" = "name.x"
  )
```



```{r}
# Define violent vs property offenses
violent <- c("HOMICIDE", "ASSAULT W/DANGEROUS WEAPON", "SEX ABUSE", "ROBBERY")
property <- c("BURGLARY", "THEFT/OTHER", "THEFT F/AUTO", "MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT", "ARSON")

# Aggregate to tract x month x year combinations
master_new <- master |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  # Remove missing tract names
  filter(!is.na(tract)) |>
  group_by(tract, year, shift) |>
  summarise(
    # Outcome variables
    total_crimes   = n(),
    violent_crimes = sum(offense %in% violent),
    property_crimes = sum(offense %in% property),
    
    # Metro variables
    dist_to_nearest_metro     = first(dist_to_nearest_metro),
    stations_within_400m         = first(stations_within_400m),
    stations_within_800m         = first(stations_within_800m),
    nearest_station     = first(nearest_station),
    above_ground        = first(above_ground),
    transfer_station    = first(transfer_station),
    red_line            = first(red_line),
    green_line          = first(green_line),
    yellow_line         = first(yellow_line),
    blue_line           = first(blue_line),
    orange_line         = first(orange_line),
    silver_line         = first(silver_line),
    
    # Census socioeconomic controls (constant within tract-year)
    total_population         = first(total_population),
    median_household_income  = first(median_household_income),
    pct_poverty              = first(pct_poverty),
    pct_unemployed           = first(pct_unemployed),
    pct_bachelors_only       = first(pct_bachelors_only),
    pct_graduate             = first(pct_graduate),
    total_housing_units      = first(total_housing_units),
    pct_vacant             = first(pct_vacant),
    pct_renter    = first(pct_renter),
    pct_black_nh  = first(pct_black_nh),
    pct_hispanic      = first(pct_hispanic),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


### Build Finalized Dataset
```{r}
# Create a tract x year x shift skeleton
panel <- expand.grid(
  tract = unique(tract_proximity_to_metro$tract),
  year  = 2020:2024,
  shift = unique(master$shift)
) |> as_tibble()

# Census controls
census_controls <- census |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  # Select relevant columns
  dplyr::select(tract, year, total_population, median_household_income,
                log_pop, starts_with("pct_"))

# Join everything
nb_data <- panel |>
  left_join(master_new, by = c("tract", "year", "shift")) |>
  mutate(
    # If crime count is NA, convert to zero
    total_crimes    = replace_na(total_crimes, 0),
    violent_crimes  = replace_na(violent_crimes, 0),
    property_crimes = replace_na(property_crimes, 0)
  ) |>
  # Select relevant columns
  dplyr::select(tract, year, shift, total_crimes, violent_crimes, property_crimes, nearest_station) |>
  left_join(tract_proximity_to_metro, by = "tract") |>
  left_join(census_controls |> arrange(tract), by = c("tract", "year")) |>
  mutate(
    year      = factor(year),
    shift     = factor(shift),
    red_line    = as.integer(str_detect(tolower(line), "red")),
    green_line  = as.integer(str_detect(tolower(line), "green")),
    yellow_line = as.integer(str_detect(tolower(line), "yellow")),
    blue_line   = as.integer(str_detect(tolower(line), "blue")),
    orange_line = as.integer(str_detect(tolower(line), "orange")),
    silver_line = as.integer(str_detect(tolower(line), "silver"))) |> 
  mutate(
    blue_orange_silver_line = as.integer(blue_line == 1 | orange_line == 1 | silver_line == 1),
    green_yellow_line = as.integer(green_line == 1 | yellow_line == 1)
  )



```


```{r}
# Write finalized dataset to a CSV file
# write_csv(nb_data, "nb_data.csv")
```
